response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically capable polyglot developer with 15+ years of experience spanning web, embedded, and open-source systems, but he falls short on several hard requirements for this role. Most critically, his German is self-described as 'Basic' — far below the C1 minimum explicitly required for a client-facing leadership position in a German IT consulting firm. Beyond the language gap, the resume reveals a career spent primarily as an individual contributor with no explicit evidence of team leadership, project management, or client advisory work. There is also no mention of UML, IT security/risk/compliance, or requirements management — all listed as required qualifications.
strengths: Broad polyglot technical background (TypeScript, D, Node.js, C++, Swift, Python-adjacent stacks) with genuine depth in Linux/Unix environments and open-source ecosystems

Demonstrated open-source leadership through GISCollective, a production geo-data platform used by real-world NGOs, with a FOSS4G conference presentation showing communication and knowledge-sharing ability
Strong software-craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, DRY principles) across multiple high-throughput production systems, indicating architectural awareness
concerns: German listed as 'Basic' — disqualifying against the mandatory C1 minimum for a client-facing Head of Development role in a German-market consultancy
No evidence of team leadership, mentoring, or people management; every role described is individual-contributor work with no direct reports or team ownership mentioned
No mention of UML, formal requirements management, IT security/risk/compliance, or client advisory experience — all explicitly required qualifications for this role

key_factors.factor: Absence of leadership and team management experience
Deep open-source and Linux technical background
